problem_text: Classify sum_{n=1}^infinity (-1)^n/n^2 as absolutely convergent, conditionally convergent, or divergent.

content.label: Alternating series form
content.applies_when: the terms alternate in sign: a_n = (-1)^n b_n with b_n = 1/n^2 > 0.

content.meaning: if sum |a_n| converges, the series converges absolutely (and hence converges); test absolute convergence first.
sum 1/n^p converges iff p > 1; sum 1/n^2 has p = 2 and converges.

content.label: test sum |(-1)^n/n^2| = sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p = 2)
content.action: test sum |(-1)^n/n^2| = sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p = 2)
content.purpose: check whether the series of absolute values converges

content.label: Absolutely convergent
content.applies_when: the series of absolute values converges, so the original series is absolutely convergent.

content.label: since sum |a_n| converges, the series is absolutely convergent (and therefore convergent)
content.action: since sum |a_n| converges, the series is absolutely convergent (and therefore convergent)
